If we build another genesis, it can only be similarly good. It can't be actually much better.

It can slightly look nicer, but you're bounded a couple percentage points either direction.

If we're going to build something great or much better, it has to be different. Simpler, but different.

To make development and testing easier, we have released a test token called 402S (402 Swap Token). 402S is an experimental token designed specifically for the 402Swap demo, used to showcase the full x402 payment flow, SettlementHook settlement logic, and the complete process of payment-driven on-chain execution.
Developers can use 402S to experience an integrated workflow—from payment authorization and settlement to protocol calls—without incurring any real asset risk during testing.

https://t.co/sr1KtCjA0i

Note: This is not financial advice. The token’s sole purpose is to demonstrate functionality.
Phase 1 testing completed successfully. Next up: integrating with @CoinbaseDev. Catch you later!

x402 v2 SettlementHook is redefining the on-chain payment experience.
In the past, users had to complete a payment first and then initiate a separate protocol interaction. Now, SettlementHook makes it possible to combine payment and execution into a single atomic transaction.
In this demo, which I built independently, I show how a USDC payment and a Uniswap swap can be merged into one transaction, achieving a true “Pay & Execute” flow.
https://t.co/f7tDZNi0fi

As more tokens supporting EIP-3009 join the ecosystem, x402 has the potential to become a universal payment layer for on-chain applications—enabling any payment to directly trigger on-chain actions and providing infrastructure for AI agents, automated workflows, and machine-to-machine payments.
When the CEO of Google agrees with your thesis, you're on the right track. \n\nAI is changing how we work, but PayAI is changing how we get paid. ������ https://t.co/EoJrH3gtQ9
